title: Cat Coffee Ground Ear Discharge: Ear Mites and Triage Guide
meta_desc: Discover why your cat has coffee ground ear discharge. Learn about ear mites, why it is a low-urgency issue, and how a photo helps your vet.
content_html:
What is Coffee Ground Discharge?

If you notice a dark, crumbly, or gritty substance inside your cat's ears that looks like coffee grounds, it is frequently a sign of an ear mite infestation (Otodectes cynotis). This debris is composed of earwax, inflammatory fluids, and the waste products of the mites. In some cases, a similar appearance can be caused by a chronic yeast or bacterial infection, which leads to the buildup of dark wax.

Is This an Emergency?

The urgency level for coffee ground discharge is Low. While this condition is very itchy, irritating, and uncomfortable for your cat, it is not a life-threatening emergency. You should schedule a veterinary appointment within the next 48 to 72 hours. Avoid using over-the-counter treatments until a vet confirms the eardrum is intact, as some medications can be toxic if the drum is ruptured.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

When you contact your veterinarian, providing a clear, close-up photo of the ear canal and the discharge can be extremely helpful. A photo allows the triage team to see the volume of debris and the level of redness or swelling in the ear flap. This helps them determine if your cat needs to be squeezed in sooner or if a standard appointment is appropriate. It also provides a baseline to track the effectiveness of treatment later on.

faqs.question: Are ear mites contagious to other pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, ear mites are highly contagious among cats and dogs. If you have multiple pets, it is likely they will all need to be checked or treated to prevent the mites from passing back and forth.

faqs.question: Can I clean my cat's ears before the appointment?
faqs.answer: It is actually better to leave the ears as they are until the veterinarian sees them. The vet will need to take a sample of the discharge for a microscopic exam to identify the specific type of mite or bacteria present.

faqs.question: Can humans catch ear mites from cats?
faqs.answer: While extremely rare, humans can occasionally experience a temporary skin rash if they are in close contact with an infested cat, but the mites cannot survive or reproduce on human skin.
